questions.question: Find the smallest number which when divided by 40, 50, 70 and 80 leaves the remainder 28, 38, 58 and 68 respectively.
questions.explanation: Required number will be the LCM of (40, 50, 70, 80) - 12 Here 12 is the common difference between the divisor and the remainder. Required number = 2800 - 12 = 2788.

questions.question: Odds in favour of A speaking the truth is 1 : 2 and odds against B speaking the truth 2 : 3. What is the probability that while stating a fact both will contradict each other?
questions.explanation: Probability that A will speak the truth = 1/3 Probability that B will lie = 2/5 Case-1 Probability that A will speak truth and B will speak lie = 1/3 X 2/5=2/15 Case-2 Probability that A will lie and B will speak truth = 2/3 X 3/5=6/15 Hence required probability = 2/15+6/15=8/15

questions.question: 2x3 - 3x2 + 5x + 3k + k2 is divisible by (x + 1). What is the sum of possible values of k?
questions.explanation: f(x) = 2x3 - 3x2 + 5x + 3k + k2 is divisible by (x + 1). Thus, by factor theorem f(−1) = 0. ∴ 2(−1)3 - 3(−1)2 + 5(−1) + 3k + k2 = 0 => k2 + 3k - 10 = 0 => k2 + 5k - 2k - 10 = 0 => (k+5)(k−2) = 0 => k =2 or −5. Sum of possible values of k = 2 - 5 = −3

questions.question: If (a−b) = p, (b−c) = q and (c−a) = r, then what is the value of (p3+q3+r3)/(abp + bcq + car)?
questions.explanation: (a−b) = p, (b−c) = q and (c−a) = r ∴ p + q + r = a−b + b−c + c−a = 0. We know if p + q + r =0, then p3+q3+r3 = 3pqr = 3(a−b)(b−c)(c−a) = 3(ab−ac−b2+bc)(c−a) = 3(abc−a2b−ac2+a2c−b2c+ab2 +bc2−abc) = 3[ab(b−a)+bc(c−b)+ca(a−c)] = −3[ab(a−b)+bc(b−c)+ca(c−a)] = −3(abp+bcq+car) ∴ (p3+q3+r3)/(abp+bcq+car) = −3

questions.question: If 3x + 1/4x = 5, then find the value of 44(5+√3)x/(2√3x - 1)2?
If (x6 + x4 + x2 + 1) = 21x3, then what is the value of (x3 + 1/x3)?
Evaluate: log25343 x log493125

Three persons A, B & C start running from the same point at 4, 6 and 8 kmph respectively. B starts one and a half hour after A. How long after B must C start such that all three meet at the same instant?
questions.explanation: B started 90 minutes after A. Distance covered by A in 90 minutes = 4 x 1.5 = 6 km Relative speed of B with respect to A = 6-4 = 2 km/h Hence he will match the difference of 6 km in 6/2 = 3 hours. Total distance covered = 3 x 6 = 18 km C will cover 18 km in 18/8 = 2.25 hours. Hence C must start 2.25-1.5 = 0.75 hours = 45 minutes after B.

questions.question: A rectangular box consists of a side of length 23.5 cm and a diagonal of length 61.1 cm. Find the length of the boundary around the rectangular box.
questions.explanation: Length of the first side = √(61.1)2-(23.5)2 = √(3733.21 - 552.25) = √3180.96 = 56.4 cm Now, boundary around the boundary= Perimeter of the rectangle= 2 (l+b) = 2(56.4+23.5) cm = 159.8 cm

questions.question: If the A(4, 3), B(2, k), and C(6, 7) are collinear, then k = ?
In which brand was there a regular decrease in sales?
questions.explanation: In Amul brand there was a regular decrease in sales as it was 1400 in 2011, 1000 in 2012 and 600 in 2013 and 500 in 2014 Hence, Option 1 is correct.

questions.question: The percentage of sales in Saras brand in 2011 was?
questions.explanation: Sales of Milk in all the 3 brands in 2011 - = 1400 + 1300 + 1200 = 3900 Sales in Saras brand in 2011 = 1300 ∴ required percentage = (1300/3900) * 100= 33.33 Hence, Option 3 is correct.

questions.question: The total sales in 2012 were approximately how many times that of Mother Dairy in the same year?
questions.explanation: Total sales in 2012 = 1000 + 1200 + 1400 = 3600 ∴ Required Answer = 3600/1400 = 2.57 times Hence, Option 2 is correct.

questions.question: What was the percent decrease in Saras sales from the year 2011-2014?
questions.explanation: Sales of Saras in 2011= 1300 Sales of Saras in 2014= 800 ∴ Percent decrease= ((1300-800)/1300) * 100 = 38.5% Hence, Option 3 is correct.

questions.question: A sum of money is lent out at compound interest rate of 40% per annum for 2 years. It would fetch Rs 3408 more if interest is compounded half-yearly. Find the sum.
In a circle AB is diameter and points C and D are on the circumference such that ∠CAD = 40° and ∠CBA = 60°. What is the value of ∠ACD?
questions.explanation: According to figure above, ∠CAD and ∠CBD subtend the same arc DC on the circumference. => ∠CAD = ∠CBD = 40° Also, ∠DBA = ∠CBA - ∠CBD = 60 - 40 = 20°. Now ∠DBA and ∠ACD subtend the same arc DA on the circumference of the circle. => ∠ACD = ∠DBA = 20°.

questions.question: 3tanA + 9sec2A = 29, then find the value of sinA, if A is an acute angle.
questions.explanation: 3tanA + 9(1 + tan2A) = 29 3tanA + 9 + 9tan2A = 29 let tanA = x 3x + 9x2 = 20 On solving, we get x = 4/3 or -5/3 But for acute angle A, tanA = 4/3 Therefore sinA = 4/5

questions.question: The average age of 12 students in a library is decreased by 3 months when two of them aged 13 years and 17 years are replaced by two new students. The average age of those new students is:
questions.explanation: Decrease = ( 12 X 3 ) months = 36 months. = 3 years Total age of new students = ( 13 + 17 ) yrs. - 3 yrs. = 30 yrs. - 3 years = 27 years. Average age of new students = 13 years 6 months.

questions.question: If cos2a+cos4a=1, what is the value of tan2a+tan4a
questions.explanation: cos2a+cos4a=1 or, cos4a=1-cos2a or, cos4a=sin2a or, cos2a X cos2a = sin2a or, cos2a=(sin2a/cos2a) or, cos2a=tan2a Now, tan2a+tan4a =tan2a+(tan2a)2 = cos2a+cos4a =1 ;

questions.question: Which of the following is equal to cosec3 A - cosec2 A - cosec A + 1?
questions.explanation: cosec3 A - cosec2 A - cosec A + 1 = cosec2 A(cosec A - 1) - 1(cosec A - 1) = (cosec2 A - 1)(cosec A - 1) = cot2 A(cosec A-1)

questions.question: Manas and Waheeb completes a project in 45 days and 40 days respectively. They began to do the work together but Manas left after some days and then Waheeb completed the remaining part of the project in 15 days. The number of days after which Manas left the project was :-
questions.explanation: Manas can do 1/45th of the project in 1 day. Waheeb can do 1/40th of the project in 1 day. Together they can do 17/360th of the project in a day. No. Of days required by them together to complete the project= 360/17. In 15 days, Waheeb would have done 15/40x100= 37.5 % of the project. So, Manas and Waheeb would have worked together for 62.5% of the project Therefore, number of days after Manas left = 360/17 x 62.5% = 13.24 days. Hence, option 2.

questions.question: The circumradius of a right triangle of perimeter 112 cm is 25 cm. find the area of the triangle.
questions.explanation: Since the triangle is right angled, hypotenuse is twice the circumradius i.e. 50 cm Let the two perpendicular sides be a & b. a+b+50 = 112 => a+b = 62 Also, a2 + b2 = 502 = 2500 (a+b)2 = a2 + b2 + 2ab => 3844 = 2500 + 2ab => ab = 672 Area = ½ ab = 336 sq cm

questions.question: The cost of 3 pens and 5 pencils is Rs.100. Also the cost of one pen and one pencil is in the ratio of 5:1 respectively. What is the cost of one dozen pens?
questions.explanation: Let the cost of the one pencil be x, The cost of one pen will be 5x, Then, 3*5x + 5x = 100 x = 5 Cost of one dozen pens = 12*5*5 = Rs.300

questions.question: ABCD is a square M is the midpoint of AB and N is the mid point of BC. DM and AN are joined and they meet at O. Then which of the following is correct ?
questions.explanation: In triangles ADM and BAN, AD = BA (Sides of the square) ∠DAM = ∠ABN = 90o AM = BN = (half of the sides) Hence the triangles are congruent. Therefore, ∠ADM = ∠BAN
